posts.title: The Lost Legacy: Unraveling the Grandeur and Tragic Demise of the Library of Alexandria

pinterest.title: The Lost Legacy: Unraveling the Grandeur and Tragic Demise of the Library of Alexandria
pinterest.description: The Library of Alexandria, founded in the 3rd century BC, was the largest and most significant library of the ancient world, serving as a beacon of knowledge and research. It housed hundreds of thousands of scrolls, attracting scholars from across the Hellenistic world. posts.title: Your Brain Uses the Same Circuits for Physical and Social Pain

pinterest.title: Social Pain is Real Pain: Your Brain Doesn't Lie
pinterest.description: Ever felt a literal ache from heartbreak or exclusion? Neuroimaging says your brain processes social pain just like physical pain, activating the same regions. It's not just in your head – it's in your neurons. Even Tylenol might help! Learn more about the science of social pain. #psychology #neuroscience #brainscience #socialpain

bluesky.text: Your brain doesn't differentiate between a stubbed toe and social rejection. The same neural circuits light up for both physical and social pain. This explains why exclusion *literally* hurts, and why painkillers can sometimes ease the sting. Mind blown.

twitter.text: Heartbreak, exclusion, rejection – they cause *real* pain because your brain uses the same circuits for social and physical pain. Neuroimaging confirms it. Acetaminophen can even reduce the sting. Science is wild. https://surfaced-x.pages.dev/item/your-brain-uses-the-same-circuits-for-physical-and-social-pain
